deadline_notes: 80hr per 2-year period. Next active deadline: December 31, 2027.
notes: 2026 new rule: 12hr/day CPE cap for any single day. 100% self-study permitted.

deadline_notes: CPE period: October 1 – September 30. License renewal deadline: December 1 (grace to December 31).
notes: 8hr A&A applies to ALL active CPAs — no attest-only carve-out. Behavioral cap: 12hr/yr. Nano-learning cap: 10hr/yr.

deadline_notes: Annual 40hr minimum plus rolling 120hr/3-year total requirement.
notes: Rolling 120hr / 3-year lookback in addition to 40hr annual minimum. 80% self-study cap is the most permissive among annual-deadline states.

deadline_notes: Deadline falls on the last business day of the CPA's birth month every 2 years.
notes: 1 of 4 ethics hours must cover AZ-specific content (ASBA Rule R4-1-453). No pre-approval registry — providers self-certify. No carryover allowed.

deadline_notes: Deadline is the last day of the CPA's birth month every 2 years.
notes: 20hr annual minimum (non-deferrable). 50% self-study cap = 40hr max per cycle. Attest CPAs need 20hr governmental/other specific subjects if applicable.

deadline_notes: Biennial (2-year) cycle ending December 31 of even years (next active: December 31, 2026).
notes: No self-study cap — Colorado's key differentiator. Attest CPAs: 8hr A&A required within the 80hr. NASBA-registered providers accepted.

deadline_notes: CPE completion period: July 1 – June 30. License renewal reporting: December 31. Effective CPE deadline is June 30.
notes: Two-date structure: CPE completion by June 30, license renewal report by December 31. 8hr financial-statement-prep requirement for attest CPAs. 20hr carryover allowed.

deadline_notes: Biennial ending December 31 of even years (next active: December 31, 2026).
notes: Unusual credit caps: firm meetings max 25% of total; publications/writing max 25%; instruction max 50%. No annual minimum.

deadline_notes: Biennial ending June 30 of odd years (next active: June 30, 2027).
notes: Rare dual mandate: 8hr A&A + 8hr Taxation required for all licensees. 4hr DE-specific ethics. 30% self-study cap (24hr max). Renewal via DELPROS. Among strictest state-specific requirements.

deadline_notes: Biennial ending June 30 of even years (next standard: June 28, 2028; Oct 2026 cohort varies).
notes: 4hr FL Board-approved ethics covering Chapters 455 & 473 — generic NASBA ethics does NOT qualify. 8hr A&A applies to ALL CPAs. 20hr behavioral cap.

deadline_notes: Biennial ending December 31 of odd years (next active: December 31, 2027).
notes: DISTINCTIVE: No ethics CPE mandate (one of very few states). 16hr A&A required for ALL licensees — not just attest CPAs. 15hr carryover (not for A&A). 20hr annual floor.

deadline_notes: Biennial ending December 31 of odd years (next active: December 31, 2027).
notes: Generous 40hr carryover. 2026 is off-cycle — deadline is December 31, 2027.

deadline_notes: Triennial (3-year) period. License renewal: June 30. Two period options: January 1–December 31 or July 1–June 30 (unique flexibility).
notes: Unique: CPAs can choose Jan-Dec or Jul-Jun period orientation. 8hr A&A for compilation CPAs only (not all attest). 24hr non-technical cap. 50% self-study cap (60hr max).

deadline_notes: Rolling 2-year window. CPE completion by December 31; license renewal reporting by January 31.
notes: Rolling 2-year window (not fixed biennial blocks). 30hr/yr annual minimum. 50hr/yr cap on CPE in any single year. License renewal by June 30.

deadline_notes: Triennial period: October 1, 2024 – September 30, 2027.
notes: Separate 1hr sexual harassment prevention training (not counted within 120hr total). Tiered self-study cap: 80hr for public accountants, 60hr for others. No annual minimum. No A&A mandate for attest. ~25K IL CPAs.

deadline_notes: Current period: January 1, 2024 – December 31, 2026. Next: January 1, 2027 – December 31, 2029.
notes: 20hr annual minimum cannot be deferred to later years — must complete each calendar year. 4hr Indiana-specific ethics required. 36-month certificate retention requirement.

deadline_notes: Biennial. Certificate number determines year: even cert# → June 30 of even years (incl. 2026 ACTIVE); odd cert# → June 30 of odd years.
notes: Staggered by certificate number. 30% personal development cap (24hr max). LMPA distinction: local municipality CPAs need 16hr/yr with 8hr municipal A&A. 8hr penalty for non-compliance.

deadline_notes: Biennial; license number determines active year. Odd license#: Jan 1, 2025 – Dec 31, 2026 (ACTIVE). Even license#: Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027.
notes: 80hr if ≥3000 engagement hours; 60hr otherwise. 16hr A&A for attest CPAs (8hr/yr). Personal development cap: 8hr for 60-hr track / 12hr for 80-hr track. Behavioral Ethics & HR/Personnel EXCLUDED from CPE.

deadline_notes: Annual renewal December 31. Rolling 2-year window (not fixed annual blocks).
notes: Rolling 80hr/2yr total; 20hr annual minimum. 3hr Board-approved ethics required in EVEN years only. No nano-learning allowed. No carryover.

deadline_notes: Biennial. License number determines year: even-number → June 30 of even years (incl. 2026 ACTIVE); odd-number → June 30 of odd years.
notes: No self-study cap, no annual minimum, no carryover, no A&A mandate. Teaching cap: 40hr; articles/publications cap: 20hr. First-renewal proration: 80/70/60/50hr by quarter. $161 renewal fee.

deadline_notes: Biennial ending December 31 of even years (next active: December 31, 2026).
notes: Generous 80hr carryover (100% of cycle total). NASBA CPE Audit Service enrollment required as of September 2025 for a percentage of licensees.

deadline_notes: CPE period: October 1 – September 30. Annual license renewal by September 30.
notes: 4hr ethics per 3-year period (not every year). 20hr carryover allowed. No A&A mandate, no self-study cap.

deadline_notes: Annual CPE by June 30. Biennial license renewal by July 31 via MiPLUS.
notes: 8hr A&A applies to ALL CPAs — distinctive compared to most states. 1 of 2 ethics hours must cover MI-specific statutes and rules. 50% self-study cap (20hr max). ~38K MI CPAs.

deadline_notes: Triennial. CPE year: July 1 – June 30. License renewal by June 30. Rolling 3-year lookback.
notes: 8hr ethics per cycle (double the 4hr national norm). 60hr technical minimum (50%). 24hr group/blended learning minimum. 72hr approved-sponsor minimum. No carryforward. Carryback allowed with fees. ~25K MN CPAs.

deadline_notes: Annual CPE by December 31.
notes: 2hr ethics annual requirement. 50% self-study cap. NASBA-registered providers accepted.

deadline_notes: CPE period: July 1 – June 30. CPE reporting deadline: August 1. License renewal: January 1.
notes: Three-date confusion: CPE period ends June 30, reporting due August 1, license renewal January 1. 1hr of ethics must cover Mississippi Public Accountancy Law & Regulations. QAS-approved self-study only. 20hr personal development cap. 20hr carryover (not for ethics, does not reduce annual minimum). Rolling 120hr/3yr also applies.

deadline_notes: Annual renewal December 31. Rolling 3-year lookback (120hr/3yr).
notes: DISTINCTIVE: No self-study cap — 100% self-study permitted (nationally rare). 2hr ethics within 60hr technical minimum. No pre-approval list required. 100% late fee penalty. NASBA CPE Audit Service.

deadline_notes: Annual CPE by December 31.
notes: 50% self-study cap. Attest services require additional A&A-related CPE. Annual cycle with no annual minimum separate from the 40hr total.

deadline_notes: CPE period: July 1 – June 30. Annual license renewal. Rolling 120hr/3yr also applies.
notes: 6hr ethics per 3-year period for CPAs who 'hold out' to the public — above the 4hr national norm. Late renewal: $185 fee (Jul 31–Aug 31). Hard August 31 expiry — license actually lapses after Aug 31 (not just a late fee). Rolling 120hr/3yr.

deadline_notes: Biennial. Birth year determines active cycle: even birth year → Dec 31, 2026 ACTIVE; odd birth year → Dec 31, 2027.
notes: Staggered by birth year (not license number). 50% self-study cap (40hr max). 16hr personal development cap. CPE reporting via Certemy by January 31. June 30 permit renewal. No carryover.

deadline_notes: Triennial. Last-name group determines year: A-F → 2026; G-M → 2027; N-Z → 2028.
notes: Staggered by last name across 3 years. 60hr carryover. No self-study cap. 2023 change: new licensees use 2-year period instead of 3-year.

deadline_notes: Triennial. Current period: January 1, 2024 – December 31, 2026.
notes: No self-study cap, no annual minimum, no carryover. Attest CPAs need additional A&A-related content.

deadline_notes: Annual renewal. Deadline falls in the CPA's birth month (personalized).
notes: DISTINCTIVE: 80% technical floor — 96 of 120 hours must be technical subjects (accounting, auditing, BL, economics, finance, govt, IT, mgmt services, regulatory ethics, statistics, tax). Stricter than most states. 24hr external-sponsor rule since 2007: cannot be 100% firm-internal CPE. Rolling 3-year window. Nano-learning 8hr sub-cap. ~10% annual audit rate.

deadline_notes: Annual CPE by December 31. License permit renewal by January 31.
notes: 8hr A&A for attest services only (audit/review/full-disclosure compilation). Tax and industry CPAs are exempt from A&A requirement. No carryover. NASBA-approved providers.

deadline_notes: Annual CPE by December 31. Two-option system: 40hr general or 24hr concentrated.
notes: Dual-option: 40hr across multiple subject areas OR 24hr if all concentrated in one recognized subject area. Ethics: 4hr per 3-year period. Attest supervisor rule for CPAs supervising attest work. No carryover. ~57K+ NY CPAs.

deadline_notes: Triennial. Current period: January 1, 2024 – December 31, 2026.
notes: 3hr PSR (Professional Standards & Responsibilities) ethics — Board-approved providers only. Attest CPAs: 24hr A&A + tax subject requirement. 8hr daily live CPE cap. No self-study cap.

deadline_notes: Annual renewal. Deadline falls in the CPA's birth month. Rolling 3-year 120hr total.
notes: Rolling 120hr/3yr total. Permit-holder technical minimum: 20hr/yr must be technical subjects. Compilation practitioners: 4hr/yr compilation CPE or Board-approved peer review. 2025 rule change: sub-1-hour increments now accepted.

deadline_notes: Biennial. Permit number determines year: even → even years; odd → odd years. Staggered half each year.
notes: DISTINCTIVE: 24hr annual minimum — above national norm. Oregon-specific ethics content required (generic NASBA may not qualify). 16hr non-technical cap — strictest in the US. NASBA National Registry accepted.

deadline_notes: Biennial ending December 31 of odd years (next active: December 31, 2027).
notes: 4hr ethics: 2hr PA state law + 2hr general ethics. 24hr A&A for attest CPAs. 20hr annual minimum. Large CPA population (Philadelphia + Pittsburgh). ~37K+ PA CPAs.

deadline_notes: Triennial. Staggered by last name: A-E → 2027; F-M → 2028; N-Z → June 30, 2026 (ACTIVE).
notes: 6hr ethics per cycle — above the 4hr national norm. 80hr self-study cap (67% of 120hr). No annual minimum, no A&A mandate, no carryover. New licensees: 80hr first cycle with 4hr ethics. $375 renewal fee.

deadline_notes: Annual CPE by December 31. License renewal by February 1.
notes: NASBA CPE Audit Service required. 20hr carryover. 12hr daily CPE cap. No self-study cap.

deadline_notes: Annual CPE by August 1 (renewal deadline). CPE period: July 1 – June 30.
notes: DISTINCTIVE: No ongoing ethics CPE requirement — only initial AICPA ethics exam for licensure. Widely misreported by aggregators. Rolling 120hr/3yr. 100% self-study OK. 24hr non-technical cap/3yr for public accountants. $100 late fee after August 1. 90-day extension available once.

deadline_notes: Biennial. License number determines year: even license# → even years (incl. 2026 ACTIVE); odd license# → odd years.
notes: 2hr TN-specific ethics — must cover TN statutes (generic NASBA ethics does NOT qualify). 24hr carryover. No self-study cap. Staggered by license number.

deadline_notes: Annual renewal. Deadline falls in the CPA's birth month. Rolling 3-year 120hr total.
notes: TSBPA-approved ethics only (state-specific). 50% technical minimum. Rolling 120hr/3yr. ~84K TX CPAs — one of the largest CPA populations. No self-study cap.

deadline_notes: Biennial ending December 31 of even years (next active: December 31, 2026).
notes: 4hr ethics: 1hr UT-specific laws & rules + 3hr general AICPA/business ethics. 40hr carryover. No A&A mandate. $63 DOPL renewal fee. ~6,300 UT CPAs.

deadline_notes: Triennial. Current period: January 1, 2024 – December 31, 2026.
notes: 20hr annual minimum (non-deferrable). 50% self-study cap. VBOA-approved ethics required.

deadline_notes: Biennial ending July 31 of odd years. Period: August 1, 2025 – July 31, 2027.
notes: 8hr A&A applies to ALL licensees (hard rule). 10hr carryover (not for A&A/ethics). Combined self-study+instructor+articles cap: 64hr (80%). Nano learning in fractional increments. $220 renewal fee. ~1,500-2,500 VT CPAs.

deadline_notes: Triennial. Rolling 3-year window. Annual minimum of 20hr applies every year.
notes: 4hr WA-approved ethics — Board-approved providers only. No self-study cap (notable advantage). Non-technical subjects and nano-learning limits apply.

deadline_notes: Biennial ending December 14 (per statute § 440.08(2)). Period: December 15, 2025 – December 14, 2027.
notes: DISTINCTIVE: December 14 deadline (not December 31). 40hr formal/instructor-led minimum — unusual half-must-be-formal requirement. 20hr annual minimum. 40hr carryover. No A&A mandate.

deadline_notes: CPE completion by December 31. License renewal reporting by January 31. License renewal (permit) by June 30.
notes: Three-date structure: CPE Dec 31, reporting Jan 31, permit renewal Jun 30. Rolling 120hr/3yr. 20hr annual minimum non-deferrable.

deadline_notes: Annual CPE by December 31. Rolling 3-year 120hr total.
notes: 4hr Board-approved WY-specific ethics (WYSCPA/CPEthink/AccountingTools only, 80% pass score required). 80hr technical minimum (67%). No carryover. Optional 90-day extension ($150 fee, to March 31, elected at renewal). Penalty CPE: lesser of shortage or 16hr. Graduated new licensee rules (0/60/120). No annual minimum (only rolling 3yr).
